---
title: "Setup Check"
format: html
---

# Goal

Use this page before class, or at the start of Module 1, to confirm that your
laptop can render Quarto documents and read the course data.

Do not spend more than 10 minutes stuck alone. If one check fails, copy the
error message, note which step failed, and ask an instructor or neighbor.

# What you need installed

| Tool | Minimum for this course | How to check |
|---|---:|---|
| R | 4.4.1 or newer | Run `R.version.string` in the R console |
| RStudio or Positron | Recent desktop version | Open a `.qmd` file and find the Render button |
| Quarto | 1.6.0 or newer | In a terminal, run `quarto --version` |
| Course files | Current repository ZIP or Git checkout | You can see `SISMID-2026.Rproj`, `data/`, `modules/`, and `exercises/` |

# Install course packages

Run this in the R console. It is safe to run again if some packages are already
installed.

```r
install.packages(c(
  "tidyverse",
  "here",
  "janitor",
  "readxl",
  "rio",
  "ggpubr",
  "printr",
  "knitr",
  "boot",
  "deSolve"
))
```

Optional or take-home modules may use additional packages such as `arrow`.
Install those only if you plan to work through that material.

# Open the project

Recommended:

1. Download or clone the repository.
1. Unzip it if needed.
1. Open `SISMID-2026.Rproj`.
1. Work from the project root, the folder that contains `_quarto.yml`.

If you open an individual file without opening the project, relative paths such
as `data/QCRC_FINAL_Deidentified.xlsx` may fail.

# Run the package check

Paste this into the R console.

```r
course_packages <- c(
  "tidyverse",
  "here",
  "janitor",
  "readxl",
  "rio",
  "ggpubr",
  "printr",
  "knitr",
  "boot",
  "deSolve"
)

missing_packages <- course_packages[
  !vapply(course_packages, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)
]

missing_packages
```

What you want to see:

- `character(0)`: all checked packages are installed.
- A package name: install that package with `install.packages("package_name")`.

# Run the data check

Paste this into the R console from the project root.

```r
data_file <- here::here("data", "QCRC_FINAL_Deidentified.xlsx")

file.exists(data_file)

qcrc <- readxl::read_xlsx(data_file, sheet = "Main_Dataset")

dim(qcrc)
names(qcrc)[1:10]
```

What you want to see:

- `file.exists(data_file)` returns `TRUE`.
- `dim(qcrc)` returns a two-number result.
- `names(qcrc)[1:10]` prints variable names.

If `file.exists(data_file)` returns `FALSE`, you are probably in the wrong
working directory or you do not have the course data. Open `SISMID-2026.Rproj`
and try again.

# Render a tiny Quarto document

Create a new file named `setup-check-local.qmd` in the project root. Start it
with this YAML header:

```yaml
---
title: "Local Setup Check"
format: html
---
```

Then add three R chunks. In RStudio, use **Insert > Code Chunk** or press
Ctrl+Alt+I, then paste the code for each chunk.

Setup chunk:

```r
library(here)
library(readxl)
library(janitor)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

Data check chunk:

```r
qcrc <- readxl::read_xlsx(
  here::here("data", "QCRC_FINAL_Deidentified.xlsx"),
  sheet = "Main_Dataset"
) |>
  janitor::clean_names()

data.frame(
  rows = nrow(qcrc),
  columns = ncol(qcrc)
)
```

Plot check chunk:

```r
ggplot(qcrc, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, na.rm = TRUE) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Number of patients")
```

Click Render.

Success means:

- an HTML file opens,
- the table shows rows and columns,
- the age histogram appears.

# If the check fails

Use the [troubleshooting checklist](troubleshooting.qmd). The most common fixes
are opening the project file, installing a missing package, checking that the
data folder exists, and restarting R.
